content.full_text:
FPPL

DOWNEY TAXPAYERS UNITED
Post Office Box 39293
Downey, CA 90239

VIA TELECOPIER

May 28, 1992

Mark Morodomi, Esq.
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 "J" Street, Suite 600
Sacramento, California 95814

Re: DOWNEY TAXPAYERS UNITED
    I.D. No. 921123

Dear Mr. Morodomi:

On the telephone today, I advised you that I had drafted a letter
to FPPC regarding certain questions which I had concerning late
expenditures and late contribution reports. Enclosed is a copy of
that letter dated May 27, 1992 which has not been previously
mailed.

Also, on the telephone today, I specifically asked you regarding
the requirements for filing Form 496 given the circumstances of our
situation. The questions which I asked you earlier by telephone
today relate to Issue Number 2 in the enclosed letter.

I am sending now, by separate cover, the letter dated May 27, 1992,
to the FPPC. I am enclosing this telecopoy for your information
only with regards to my oral request. I am still very interested
in hearing from you by telephone as to the requirements for filing
Form 496 by our committee.

Very truly yours,

TREDWAY, BRANDMEYER & LUMSDAINE

By: Joseph A. Lumsdaine

JAL:bda.24

DOWNEY TAXPAYERS UNITED
Post Office Box 39293
Downey, CA 90239


May 27, 1992


Fair Political Practices Commission
428 "J" Street, Suite 600
Sacramento, California 95814

Re: DOWNEY TAXPAYERS UNITED
    I.D. No. 921123

Dear Madam or Sir:

As chairman of this political action committee, I am confused as to
the filing requirements as required by California Government Code
sections 82031, 82025, 82036 and 82036.5.  These sections pertain
primarily to Form Numbers 496 and 497 relating to late, independent
expenditures and late contribution reports.  As an attorney, I have
reviewed those code sections, as well as the forms, but still have
several questions which are set forth below, after a brief discussion
of the facts.

                    I.  THE FACTS

    Downey Taxpayers United (DTU) was formed to oppose efforts by
the City Employees Coalition (CEC) to hand-pick and endorse certain
candidates.  At the forming of DTU, the CEC had not yet endorsed any
specific candidates.  After the CEC endorsed certain specific candi-
dates, DTU decided to endorse certain other candidates.  Up to this
point, DTU has raised only $300 in contributions.

    DTU decided to put out a mailer setting forth their position
and, in the same mailer, supported and opposed certain candidates.
I contacted a printer known to me, who stated that the approximate
cost would be $6,600 for printing and mailing of the mailers.  On May
21, 1992, the printer apparently deposited the mailers with the post
office.  On Saturday, May 23, 1992, constituents in the City of
Downey began receiving copies of the mailers.  I did not discover
this until my return from out of town for the holiday, Tuesday, May
26, 1992.

    Four different types of mailers were mailed to each of the four
districts of the city.  Each mailer contained an identical statement
of position of DTU.  In one district, the mailer supported one city-
wide candidate and opposed one city-wide candidate.  In the other

Fair Political Practices Commission
May 27, 1992
Page Two


three districts, the mailer supported the same city-wide candidate
and opposed the same city-wide candidate and additionally supported
one district candidate and opposed at least one district candidate.
(In one district, two opposition candidates were opposed).

     Issue Number 1:    Is this a "slate mailer" as
referenced in Government Code section 82048.3?

     Government Code section 82048.3 specifies a "mass mailing which
supports or opposes a total of four or more candidates".  "Mass
mailing" is defined in Government Code section 82048.5 to mean
"substantially similar pieces of mail". Since each mailer for each
district supports, at most, two candidates and opposes, at most,
three candidates, my interpretation is that the mailers are not
"slate mailers" within the meaning of Government Code section
82048.3.  Otherwise, that code section should refer to a mailer which
"supports and opposes a total of four or more candidates".

     Issue Number 2:    Is a Form 496 required to be
filed at this time?

     We have not yet been invoiced by the printer, although we have
been advised that the amount is probably about $6,600 for all four
districts.

     Government Code section 82036.5 defines a "late independent
expenditure" as one which "totals in the aggregate One Thousand
Dollars ($1,000) or more and is made for or against any specific
candidate".  With a combined cost of approximately $6,600, the cost
of the mailer for each of the four districts would be approximately
$1,650.  Since each mailer supports at least one candidate and
opposes at least one candidate, I would interpret Government section
82036.5 as not requiring a filing of Form 496 because the expenditure
for or against each specific candidate would be, at most, one-half of
$1,650, or $825 for each candidate supported or opposed.

     Further, Government Code section 82025 defines expenditure as
"an enforceable promise to make payment" and further, specifies the
date of payment as "when 'payment is made or the date consideration,
if any, is received'".  Since our committee requested work to be done,
arguably, there is an enforceable promise to make payment.  However,
as to the date of the expenditure, I would understand the require-
ments of these code sections as not coming into play until an invoice
is actually received from the printer.  Otherwise, we would have to
guess as to the dollar amount to be stated for each candidate on Form
496.

Fair Political Practices Commission
May 27, 1992
Page Three


     Issue Number 3:    Is Form 497 relating to late
contributions required to be filed at this time?

     At this time, less than $300 has been raised by DTU.  We expect
to raise contributions to cover the cost of the mailers or, in the
alternative, if the invoice has been paid before all such contribu-
tions are raised, myself and certain other members of the loosely-
formed steering committee for this group would loan the money to DTU
to make payment on the invoice.  However, no such loans have been
made at this time.

     A further issue is that Government Code section 82036 specifi-
cally refers to contributions of $1,000, or more, received by "a
candidate, a controlled committee, or committee formed or existing
primarily to support or oppose a candidate".  DTU does not appear to
fall within any of those categories.  Accordingly, my conclusion is
that Form 497 is not required to be filed, certainly not at this time
because of the lack of contributions or loans.  Indeed, I would
conclude that the committee is not required to file a Form 497 at any
time since DTU is not "a candidate, a controlled committee, or a
committee formed or existing primarily to support or oppose a candi-
date".

                         II.    SUMMARY

     Rather than risk being in violation of any of the code sections, I am
asking for a definitive opinion from the FPPC and confirm the follow-
ing interpretations of the above-referenced code sections:

     1.    Form 496 need not be filed because there has been no
expenditure exceeding $1,000 for or against any specific
candidate, since, at most, DTU expended $825 for each
candidate.

     2.    If Form 496 is to be filed because of some other
interpretation of Government Code section 82036.5, Form 496
is not required to be filed until after receipt of an
invoice from the printer.

     3.    Because each mailer supports, at most, two candidates
and opposes, at most, two candidates, the mailers are not a
"slate mailer".

     4.    Form 497 is not required to be filed because DTU is
not a "candidate, controlled committee or committee formed
or existing primarily to support or oppose a candidate"
within the meaning of Government Code section 82036.

Fair Political Practices Commission
May 27, 1992
Page Four


On behalf of DTU, and the voters of the City of Downey, I thank you
for your consideration of this matter.

Very truly yours,

DOWNEY TAXPAYERS UNITED

[signature]

By: Joseph A. Lumsdaine
    Chairperson




jal/bda/dtu.22
